exemplar.title: To what extent does Indian media reflect societal attitudes and views of gender in India?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Indian media reflect societal attitudes and views of gender in India?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Clearly stated and focused research question guiding each section
Effective communication of topic and purpose throughout introduction and conclusion
Consistent thematic organization linking topic to Indian media examples
Strong conceptual foundation in themed sections (e.g., Education)
weaknesses: Methodology is only implicitâno criteria for selecting examples or analytical framework
Lacks rationale for section ordering and theme selection
Insufficient justification for using particular media texts (e.g., Padman)
Inconsistent citation details and missing in-text references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a highly focused topic and research question (band 6) with accurate and effective communication of purpose. However, the methodology is partially implicit, lacking clear selection criteria and explanation of analytical approach (band 4). Balancing these factors, a mid-upper band score of 3/6 reflects strong focus but incomplete methodological clarity.

strengths: Robust empirical support from UNICEF report and Tamil Nadu study data
Clear contextualization of Indian cultural norms in media analysis
Generally appropriate and accurate use of key terminology and concepts
Effective subject knowledge illustrated through choice of soap-opera examples
weaknesses: Depth of theoretical insight is uneven; more media theory integration needed
Several generalizations lack citations or concrete examples
Occasional informal phrasing and lapses in precision
Claims about news media and stereotypes need empirical support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of sources and subject knowledge are mostly relevant (band 4), with accurate terminology but uneven depth. The overall performance sits in the middle band of the rubric; a score of 2/6 acknowledges solid strengths while reflecting the need for more rigorous theoretical framing and citation precision.

strengths: Good relevance of a diverse range of media examples to the research question
Coherent argument linking media portrayals to societal attitudes
Insightful future research proposals showing reflective consideration
Logical structure supporting argument progression from introduction to conclusion
weaknesses: Limited use of scholarly sources; reliance on journalistic/blog-style material
Sparse triangulation and absence of counter-examples
Minimal evaluation of source reliability or representativeness
Conclusions occasionally asserted without full analytical support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates good research relevance and coherent discussion (band 7â9), but analysis depth and evaluation are only adequate (band 4â6). Overall, a middle-band score of 3/12 captures clear strengths in argumentation while noting gaps in depth, triangulation, and critical evaluation.

strengths: Complete and appropriate structure with title page, TOC, sections, conclusion, works cited
Clear layout and formatting, including distinct headers and readable fonts
Adherence to EE structural conventions enhances readability
Works Cited section is clearly demarcated and accessible
weaknesses: Table of Contents lacks page numbers for each entry
Bibliography entries split across pages and inconsistent ordering
Abrupt paragraph breaks suggest incomplete text
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and layout meet the highest rubric band (band 3â4), but minor formatting oversights (missing TOC page numbers, split entries, inconsistent citations) limit full marks. A balanced score of 2/4 reflects clear presentation with room for refinement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââTo what extent does Indian media reflect societal attitudes and views of gender in India?ââis presented verbatim on the title page and guides each body section.  Its boundaries (medium = Indian advertisements, soap operas, films; concept = reflection of gender attitudes) are sufficiently narrow for 4,000 words and are visibly connected to every discussion point, satisfying the requirements for a clearly stated and effectively focused RQ.

Sections move beyond mere summary by linking media portrayals to societal attitudes; for instance, the analysis of blue gel in pad adverts and of Tulsi Viraniâs idealised role illustrate reflective thinking.  Yet the essay rarely interrogates counter-examples, measure impact, or weigh alternative interpretations, and some conclusions are asserted without full support.  The analysis is therefore âadequateâ within the 4-6 range.

Quotations, statistics, and plot summaries from named sources (UNICEF report, Pew survey, specific TV serials, âPad Manâ, etc.) are generally relevant to the argument, showing appropriate but not always rigorous applicationâseveral claims (e.g., beauty-product stereotypes) lack citation or rely on anecdotal phrasing.  Overall use is âmostlyâ rather than âclearlyâ relevant and appropriate.

Formatting elements (title, word count, section headings, page indications within the transcript) are present.  While citations are inconsistent in style, the basic layoutâmargins, spacing, readable fontâmeets expectations.  Thus layout is âcorrectly appliedâ per the top band for this criterion.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of Indian cultural norms and media content, referencing collectivism, patrilineal descent, and soap-opera tropes accurately.  However, the discussion sometimes stays descriptive, and the depth of contextual or theoretical insight is uneven, so the knowledge and use of sources are only partially effective rather than consistently so.

The essay follows the required EE structureâtitle page with word count, table of contents, headed sections, conclusion, and works cited.  Transitions are logical and paragraphing is appropriate, so the structure is clearly suitable and complete.

The investigation assembles an adequate range of contemporary and popular media examples plus secondary data, showing clear relevance to the RQ.  Nevertheless, many sources are journalistic or blog-style, and scholarly depth is limited; triangulation between sources is sporadic.  This positions the research quality solidly in the âgood/clear relevanceâ mid-band rather than the âexcellent/consistentâ top band.

The student states a precise topicâhow far Indian media mirrors societal gender attitudesâand consistently keeps this lens in view across the three thematic sections (menstruation, family roles, education).  The purpose (to explore the degree of reflection) and cultural focus (Indian media texts) are explicitly announced in the introduction, reiterated in section headings, and returned to in the conclusion.  This clarity and relevance to an English-B category 2 investigation meet the descriptor for accurate and effective communication of topic and purpose.

A coherent line of argument runs through introduction, themed body sections, and conclusion, and claims are generally logical.  However, evaluation of limitations, reliability of sources, or the extent of representativeness is minimal.  Consequently, discussion is âeffectiveâ in places but contains notable gaps, matching the middle band rather than higher ones.

Method is mostly implicit: the essay draws on secondary sources (news articles, NGO reports, academic commentary) and specific media texts, but it does not explain criteria for selecting examples, provide a rationale for grouping them, or outline any analytical framework.  A works-cited list shows a breadth of material, yet inconsistent citation details and a lack of in-text references for some assertions indicate only partial evidence of informed selection.  Hence the work meets âmostly completeâ rather than âclear and effectiveâ methodology.

Key concepts such as âpatriarchalâ, âstigmaâ, âcommodificationâ, and âcollectivistâ are used appropriately, and gender terminology is largely accurate.  Still, occasional informal phrasing (âtrophy wifeâ, âtalk of the townâ) and some lapses in precision mean consistency is moderate, aligning with the middle band.

annotations.comment: The title is clearly stated at the start, satisfying the requirement for a concise investigation title.
While the research question is explicit, the introduction would benefit from a brief outline of the analytical approach and selection criteria to guide the reader from the outset.
Table of Contents is present and formatted clearly, aiding navigation through the investigation.
Entries in the table lack corresponding page numbers, reducing usability; include page references for each section.
The introduction demonstrates strong contextualization of Indian mediaâs impact on societal values, showing clear subject knowledge.
The transition to the Menstruation section occurs without an explanation of why sections are ordered; add rationale for how themes were selected.
Use of quantitative data from the Tamil Nadu study enhances credibility and relevance to the research question.
Some interpretations about sanitary pad advertisementsâ intent are asserted without explicit evidence or precise citation details.
The analysis of coded language like âthose daysâ demonstrates nuanced understanding of how media language reflects societal taboos.
Consider discussing viewer reception data or audience studies to substantiate how the Whisper ad reinforces gender stereotypes.
Relying on the film âPadmanâ alone limits representativeness; justify why this example is indicative of broader media trends.
The explanation of collectivism and patrilineal descent effectively sets up analysis of family roles with accurate cultural terminology.
Soap-opera examples like âKahani Ghar Ghar Kiâ and âKyuki Saansâ¦â are well-chosen to illustrate traditional family narratives.
The portrayal of Tulsi Virani is described without considering differing audience interpretations or potential subversions.
The term âtrophy wifeâ is used precisely to critique media portrayals, showing consistent use of gender terminology.
To deepen the argument, integrate a media theory frameworkâsuch as framing theoryâto support analysis of domestic role portrayals.
Claims about news media focusing on appearance need empirical data or citations to support these generalizations.
Effective use of UNICEF report data provides robust empirical support for gender representation arguments.
The introduction to the Education section links societal views and gender roles clearly, providing a strong conceptual foundation.
An abrupt paragraph break suggests incomplete text; ensure full sentences are present and properly formatted.
Inclusion of contemporary STEM workforce statistics strengthens the argument on educational disparities.
To deepen analysis, compare admission trends across multiple years or institutions rather than a single snapshot.
General statements on soap-operation reinforcement lack concrete examples; include named serials to substantiate claims.
Integrate audience reception metrics or case studies to substantiate the claimed impact of educational portrayals.
The citation for Fair & Lovely ads is incomplete and inconsistent; adopt a uniform referencing style for online sources.
The Conclusion section is clearly demarcated with a distinct header, aiding structural coherence.
Consider restating the research question and evaluation criteria explicitly in the conclusion to reinforce the investigationâs focus.
The proposal of future research directions is insightful and demonstrates reflective consideration of the investigationâs limitations.
Refine suggested follow-up research questions with specific methodologies, data sources, and sampling justifications.
The Works Cited header is clearly formatted, ensuring the bibliography is easy to locate.
Citation entries show inconsistent formatting; adopting a single style guide (e.g., MLA or APA) will improve professionalism.
Include DOIs or URLs consistently for all online sources to enhance source traceability.
A single citation entry is split across two pages, disrupting continuity; ensure list items remain intact.
Alphabetize bibliography entries by author surname to enhance navigation and academic convention compliance.
